questions.question: What is leachate and why is it a concern in landfill management? (2-3 marks)
expectedPoints: Leachate is contaminated liquid formed when water percolates through waste.
It contains dissolved and suspended materials from the decomposed waste.
It is a major concern because it can contaminate groundwater and soil if the landfill liner fails, posing risks to human health and the environment.

questions.question: Discuss the concept of Integrated Waste Management (IWM). Explain its principles and why it is considered a more sustainable approach than relying solely on landfilling. (5-10 marks)
expectedPoints: Definition: IWM is a holistic approach that uses a combination of waste management methods tailored to local conditions.
Principle 1: It follows the waste hierarchy, prioritizing reduction, reuse, and recycling over disposal.
Principle 2: It considers local factors like waste composition, technology, economics, and environmental impact.
Contrast with Landfilling: Sole reliance on landfilling is a linear model (take-make-dispose) that wastes resources, consumes land, and creates long-term pollution risks (leachate, methane).
Benefits of IWM: It is a circular model that recovers value (materials, energy), reduces environmental impact, and is more sustainable and resilient in the long term.
Conclusion: IWM provides a flexible, efficient, and environmentally superior framework for managing municipal solid waste.
